customActions.name: Debug Blast (Fire Bolt reskin)
customActions.description: Ranged spell attack. A beam of error messages hits the target. 1d10 fire damage.

customActions.name: Stack Overflow (Magic Missile reskin)
customActions.description: Three recursive function calls that always hit. 1d4+1 force damage each.

customActions.name: Firewall (Shield reskin)
customActions.description: Reaction: +5 AC until start of next turn. A holographic firewall appears.

customFeatures.name: Spellbook (GitHub Repository)
customFeatures.description: Spells stored in a private repo. Ritual casting = running the CI pipeline.

customFeatures.name: Arcane Recovery (Ctrl+Z)
customFeatures.description: Once per long rest, recover spell slots during a short rest. Requires a laptop.

customFeatures.name: Scholar (Arcana Expert)
customFeatures.description: Double proficiency on Arcana checks. Can identify magical code smell.

customFeatures.name: Overclock
customFeatures.description: Once per day, cast a spell one level higher than the slot used. CPU fan noise intensifies.

notes.personalityTraits: Types during combat. Treats spell components as npm packages. Has opinions about tabs vs spaces that border on religious.
notes.ideals: All problems are debugging problems. Vampires are just a type error in the natural order.
notes.bonds: My laptop contains a spell that could end the vampire threat, but it's in a legacy codebase with no documentation.
notes.flaws: Cannot function without coffee. Has mass-deployed Detect Magic to campus WiFi and it keeps pinging false positives on the engineering building.
notes.backstory: CS major at Baskin Engineering who accidentally compiled an actual spellbook while trying to parse a medieval manuscript for a digital humanities elective. Now writes spells in Python and casts them through a custom terminal emulator. Discovered the vampire coven when their supernatural interference kept crashing the campus network at 3am. Byte thought it was a DDoS attack. It was worse.
notes.freeform: Familiar is a mass of floating semicolons named 'Lint'. Byte insists it's a valid creature type.
